danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct vehicle sprites moving toward player or blocking road
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or destruction
Aerial threat creating crater hazards on road surface

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark destructive marks/gaps in road texture
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard that destroys player vehicle on contact

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy sprite overlapping or adjacent to player car
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent collision damage or forced road exit

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual transition to off-road terrain or water
danger_signals.meaning: Zone where player vehicle sustains cumulative damage and eventual destruction

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear scrolling path with minimal vehicle sprites
Distinct special vehicle sprite with visual differentiation
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity to acquire temporary combat advantage (oil slick, smoke screen, SAM)
Transition point offering alternate movement mode (speedboat) for tactical variety

safety_signals.visual_form: Dashboard/HUD numeric display rising while on-road
safety_signals.meaning: Player vehicle is intact and operating in safe condition

safety_signals.visual_form: HUD status change reflecting score threshold achievement
safety_signals.meaning: Player has surpassed performance milestone, gaining death buffer

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Vehicle is distinct from all enemy types
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawns on road center; persists at screen top during vertical scroll

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied enemy vehicle types (sedans, trucks, etc.)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple silhouettes indicating different attack patterns and threat profiles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across road lanes; approach from forward/rear/sides

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Visually separated from both player and armed enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Present on shared roadway; collision penalizes player score

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Overhead sprite with bomb drop animation
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unique visual distinct from ground vehicles
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears above road; bomb indicators cascade downward

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unlimited car supply during lead-in period; minimal enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player has freedom to explore and make mistakes without permanent penalty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Finite car supply; must earn extra cars by score milestones; increased enemy vehicle frequency
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must balance aggression with caution; tactical weapon deployment becomes critical

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual palette shift in road and background; new environmental features
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types and patterns may adapt; weapons van repositioned for new territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Vehicle sprite transforms to boat; water environment replaces road; oil slick becomes flamethrower visual
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement and firing patterns adjust to aquatic physics; helicopter threat persists; boat-specific enemies appear

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dashboard lights illuminate to indicate active weapon state (oil, smoke, SAM)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player can deploy limited-ammo tactical tools; visual feedback on button press confirms firing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Collision proximity visual cues; road-edge transition animations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle movement becomes increasingly constrained; cumulative off-road exposure triggers destruction

environmental_storytelling.example: Different road types (standard highway, waterway sections) signal progression through fictional spy mission geography
Multiple enemy vehicle silhouettes suggest organized opposition forces; civilian cars establish stakes (protection objective)
Periodic special-weapon supply points imply player operates in hostile territory with pre-positioned support infrastructure
Architectural transition points communicate secret agent protocol (vehicle mode conversion as evasion tactic)
Aerial opponent and bomb crater hazards suggest high-level pursuit; persistent threat across all terrain modes

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Dashboard status lights and extra-car thresholds
environmental_storytelling.example: Performance-based resource gain (extra lives) communicates narrative of escalating danger requiring improved skill

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Move toward player lane with collision intent; sprite animation suggests directional momentum
Angle toward player car edges; nudge player toward road boundary
Appears overhead; drops bomb indicators that cascade to road; craters persist as environmental hazard
Maintains lane movement; does not attack; collision triggers score penalty (visual feedback: counter halt)
Appears in new territory; dashboard light signals summoning capability; collision grants special weapon pickup
Score counter increments; responsive to steering yoke and acceleration input
Cumulative damage accrual; reduced score; visual positioning at boundary; eventual sprite destruction

notes: Spy Hunter (NES 1987) employs a top-down scrolling perspective to communicate world-state primarily through environmental visual distinction and enemy sprite differentiation rather than explicit political/faction symbolism. The game's spy-thriller paradigm (drawing from James Bond inspiration) is communicated through mechanical gameplay elements (weapons, gadgets, evasion) rather than visual faction markers. Danger is signaled through proximity mechanics (enemy vehicles, helicopter), environmental hazards (road craters, boundaries), and resource depletion (finite special weapons, limited cars). Safety is indicated by open road space, weapon van access, and score increments. The vertical-scrolling vehicular combat paradigm limits faction representation to entity type differentiation (player/enemy/civilian/helicopter). World-state shifts manifest through terrain changes, enemy density variation, and weapon availability rather than narrative visual cues.
